news.summary: President Donald Trump's recent decision to significantly increase tariffs has led to a grim downgrade in China's growth prospects. Goldman Sachs economists, including Andrew Tilton, now forecast a slow economic recovery for China with potential severe repercussions in the labor market. The abrupt tariff escalation, announced on April 9th, raised U.S. duties on Chinese products to an unprecedented 125%, placing nearly 20 million jobs reliant on exports at risk. This tariff surge is part of a retaliatory cycle with Beijing, which raised tariffs on U.S. goods to 84%. Consequently, the effective U.S. tariff rate on Chinese imports has soared from 11% to 125% since Trump took office. Goldman Sachs projects this increase alone could diminish China's real GDP by 2.6 percentage points. The escalating trade tensions between the two nations have raised significant economic stakes, with further details on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Trump's 125% tariffs on China may jeopardize up to 20M jobs, Goldman Sachs warns of sluggish growth and deep economic impact.

news.summary: There has been a setback in addressing inflation, with the Federal Reserve revising down its growth forecast for 2025. Additionally, there is a diminishing optimism among central bank officials about their ability to boost the economy through two additional interest rate reductions in the current year.
news.headline: Fed unsure of next steps as inflation progress halts. Growth expectations for 2025 faded, fewer policymakers predict 2 more rate cuts in 2021.

news.summary: In March, the annual inflation rate decreased more than anticipated, falling to 2.4% according to the latest Consumer Price Index report. Economists warn that this decline may only be temporary due to President Donald Trump's recent tariff policies, which could potentially reverse this trend by driving up consumer prices. Joseph Brusuelas, chief economist at RSM, predicts that this dip in the CPI will not last long, as tariff-driven inflation is expected to rise, with overall and core inflation potentially reaching 4% to 4.5% by mid-2025. Brusuelas highlighted the significance of the 0.1% drop in core goods prices, indicating that this may be the last decrease investors witness for a considerable period. To read more on this topic, check the full article on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Annual inflation rate decreases to 2.4% in March, but economists warn of temporary relief due to potential impact of tariffs, predicting 4-4.5% inflation by mid-2025.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to a record low of 3.8%. This marks a significant decrease from the previous rate of 4.2% and is the lowest it has been in the past decade. The report also shows that the economy has added 250,000 new jobs in the last month, surpassing analysts' expectations. Additionally, the stock market has seen a surge, with the main index reaching an all-time high of 35,000 points. This positive trend in the labor market and economy as a whole has created a sense of optimism among investors and policymakers.
news.headline: Satellogic completes transition to U.S. jurisdiction to enhance market position and investor access.

news.summary: During President Donald Trump's first term, an anti-money-laundering law was passed to target anonymous shell companies aiding financial crimes. The Trump administration is now excluding tens of millions of businesses from this law, allowing them to operate without scrutiny.
news.headline: Trump admin exempts tens of millions of companies from anti-money-laundering law intended to target anonymous shell companies.

news.summary: On March 28, 2025, in Vancouver, British Columbia, Calibre Mining Corp. (TSX: CXB; OTCQX: CXBMF) announced the filing and initiation of the mailing process of its management information circular in relation to the business combination with Equinox Gold Corp. (TSX: EQX; NYSE: EQX). This special meeting is intended for the holders of Calibre common shares and options issued under Calibre’s long-term incentive plan, collectively known as Calibre Securityholders. The arrangement involves a merger between Calibre and Equinox, and the circular contains essential details regarding the Meeting Materials for the upcoming special meeting.
news.headline: Calibre Mining files and mails Meeting Materials for Special Meeting with Equinox Gold, for business combination.

news.summary: The House Financial Services Committee is becoming a focal point for Republican political aspirations, potentially representing a critical moment for the GOP's slim House majority.
news.headline: GOP faces risks as House Financial Services Committee becomes center for Republican political ambition, impacting GOP’s majority.

news.summary: French court barred Marine Le Pen, the leader of right-wing party RN, from running in the 2027 presidential election for embezzlement of public funds. She was sentenced to four years in prison, with two years suspended and two under house arrest. France is the third NATO nation this year to bar a candidate, following Romania and Turkey. RN received 37% of votes in June, up significantly from 0.08% in 2007. Le Pen's popularity is growing, with polls showing she could easily win the first round of presidential elections with 36% of the vote. This move raises concerns about democratic erosion in the EU and the judiciary's role in electoral outcomes.
news.headline: French court bars Marine Le Pen from presidency due to embezzlement, despite popularity growth; party's votes rose to 37%. More legal challenges against right-wing populists noted across Europe and Turkey, raising concerns about democratic norms. Le Pen's RN party is gaining traction, with strong anti-immigration stance resonating with voters. Le Pen could easily win the first round of France's presidential elections, polling at 36%. Warning against judicial interference in electoral outcomes, highlighting potential erosion of democracy.

news.summary: Today marks the release of inflation data, revealing a rise in prices. Additionally, the trade conflict initiated by the current US administration continues to escalate, with tensions expected to intensify further. The situation indicates a turbulent period ahead.
news.headline: Inflation concerns rising as Trump 2.0 trade war sparks uncertainty, leading to potential stagflation.

news.summary: Investors are closely monitoring President Donald Trump's trade policies as stocks experienced a significant decline on Thursday. Concerns over the impact of his new tariff plan on the economy have led to a drop in the stock market.
news.headline: Stocks plummeted due to fears of Trump's trade wars impacting the economy heavily.

news.summary: After a strong rally on Wednesday, Wall Street saw a sharp pullback on Thursday following the news of a 90-day tariff pause. The Consumer Price Index dropped from 2.8% in February to 2.4% in March, falling below the expected 2.6% and marking the lowest reading since September 2024. Monthly inflation also dipped by 0.1%, the first decrease since May 2020. This data has increased expectations of near-term Fed rate cuts, with markets pricing in an 88% chance of a 0.25% cut by June. The S&P 500 fell by 4.8%, the Nasdaq 100 by 5.4%, and the Russell 2000 by 5.3%. Despite the market decline, traditional safe havens like Treasuries and the dollar saw increased interest. This reflects investor caution amidst ongoing tariff uncertainties.
news.headline: Wall Street tumbles, Dollar crashes, Gold hits record highs as inflation cools more than predicted- CPI falls to 2.4%, lowest since 2024. S&P 500 drops 4.8%, Nasdaq tumbles 5.4%, Russell 2000 sinks 5.3%. Markets ponder tariff drama, 88% chance of 0.25% Fed rate cut by June.

news.summary: A prominent travel marketplace and artificial intelligence (AI) technology company, Mondee, has recently undergone an acquisition and recapitalization. This move has brought significant changes to the company's structure and operations.
news.headline: Tabhi acquires Mondee, boosting liquidity and capital structure after Chapter 11. Mondee is a travel marketplace and AI company.

news.summary: Key financial industry figures on Wall Street are expressing opposition to President Donald Trump's tariff strategies. Despite their efforts, there is limited confidence among these influential individuals that their concerns are effectively resonating.
news.headline: Stock market affected by trade war chaos, Wall Street leaders oppose Trump's tariffs with unclear impact.

news.summary: Prominent figures in the financial sector were unsuccessful in influencing President Donald Trump on tariffs, while the bond market appeared to hold sway over him.
news.headline: Global trade war tensions reduced as Wall Street's efforts failed but bond market influenced Trump's decision.

news.summary: Knostic, based in Herndon, Virginia, has been selected as one of the Top 10 Finalists for the RSAC™ 2025 Conference Innovation Sandbox contest. The company specializes in providing need-to-know access controls for Generative AI and is recognized for its efforts in securing enterprise large language models (LLMs) on a large scale. Knostic will showcase its technology at the RSAC™ 2025 Conference at the Moscone Center in San Francisco on April 28th. The presentation will be made to industry judges and a live audience, marking a significant achievement for the company.
news.headline: Knostic finalist for RSAC™ 2025 Innovation Sandbox Contest for AI security, to present at Moscone Center.

news.summary: The president is hinting at agreements with several trade allies as a move to alleviate economic concerns and demonstrate the success of his tariff strategy. This development comes amidst growing anxiety over the economy and aims to showcase the effectiveness of the plan.
news.headline: Trump remains resolute on tariffs, hinting at trade deals with various partners to ease economic worries.

news.summary: U.S. stocks experienced a widespread surge on Monday, breaking a streak of volatility as President Donald Trump suggested a more diplomatic approach to tariff discussions. Trump's announcement on Truth Social revealed a surge in negotiation requests, with active talks involving South Korea and indications of China's desire for a deal. He also referenced a joint venture concerning an Alaska pipeline and military cost-sharing, noting the imminent arrival of South Korea's top negotiation team in the U.S. Simultaneously, Federal Reserve speakers began highlighting the negative economic consequences of tariffs. Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee cautioned against waiting until the end of the quarter to see a potential drop in investment and shrinking GDP growth. By midday trading in New York, all.
news.headline: Stocks rally after Trump suggests more diplomatic approach to trade negotiations. Chipmakers surge. Bitcoin lags behind. Fed concerned about tariffs' negative economic impact. Fed President Goolsbee warns of potential investment drop.

news.summary: U.S. stock futures declined on Thursday following a tariff pause, with major benchmark indices down in premarket. President Trump credited the pause to 75 non-retaliating nations' negotiation efforts. Investors are concerned about trade barriers' impact on the economy and await CPI data. Economists forecast a 2.6% annual increase in overall CPI for March 2025, lower than February's 2.8%. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 4.29%, and the two-year bond was at 3.85%. Markets predict an 79.8% chance the Fed will maintain current interest rates in May. In the previous session, S&P 500 surged by 9.52%, led by consumer discretionary and tech sectors. Notable movers included Apple, Nvidia, and Tesla, though Trump's social media post threatened a tariff hike on Chinese goods. Dow Jones soared 7.87%, S&P 500 surged 9.52%, Nasdaq Composite jumped 12.16%, and Russell 2000 leaped 8.66%. Key changes in futures were: Dow Jones -1.73%, S&P 500 -2.23%, Nasdaq 100 -2.55%, Russell 2000 -2.92%. SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust was down 3.04% at $531.92, and Invesco QQQ Trust ETF declined 2.53% to $454.20 in premarket trading.
news.headline: US stocks likely to open lower ahead of March inflation release, with S&P 500 gaining over 5%. Inflation forecasts suggest a 2.6% increase in CPI for March 2025, down from February's 2.8%. Core CPI expected to ease to 3.0% year-over-year. 10-year Treasury bond yields at 4.29%. Markets predict 79.8% likelihood of unchanged interest rates. Futures change: Dow Jones -1.73%, S&P 500 -2.23%, Nasdaq 100 -2.55%, Russell 2000 -2.92%. Index performance saw Nasdaq Composite rise by 12.16%, S&P 500 by 9.52%, Dow Jones by 7.87%, and Russell 2000 by 8.66%.

news.summary: Futures dropped significantly after China responded to Trump's tariffs, following the market's largest losses since 2020. The focus is now on the upcoming jobs report and comments from Powell. Dow Jones futures plunged due to concerns over China's retaliation, sparking fears of a recession.
news.headline: Dow Jones futures plummet due to China's retaliation against Trump tariffs, amid recession fears after worst market losses since 2020. Job report and Powell's actions are crucial.

news.summary: On April 4, 2025, Singapore-based company SIMPPLE Ltd. (NASDAQ: SPPL) revealed it has secured a new contract worth $524,000 for providing autonomous cleaning robotics to Singapore's international airport. SIMPPLE, a notable player in the facilities management industry, is recognized for its innovative technology solutions.
news.headline: SIMPPLE Ltd. secures $524,000 contract to provide autonomous cleaning robots at Singapore Airport Terminal.

news.summary: Today, the Dow Jones saw a significant drop of 1,100 points due to escalating fears surrounding the U.S.-China trade war. Tech giants such as Apple, Nvidia, and Tesla experienced a sharp decline in their stock prices. This news was reported by Investor's Business Daily.
news.headline: Dow Jones drops 1,100 points on U.S.-China trade war fears, with Apple, Nvidia, Tesla stocks falling.

news.summary: Today in Singapore, SIMPPLE Ltd. (NASDAQ: SPPL) introduced its latest robotic innovation, Orion, at Kings Club in Melbourne. The cutting-edge robot offers real-time security surveillance, on-demand digital concierge support, and intelligent spot cleaning and sweeping capabilities. This launch event, organized by Melbourne property management company Above OCM and technology integrator Australian Robot Technology, showcases SIMPPLE's Gemini and Orion robots to clients and partners. The event spans two days, inviting attendees to witness the debut of Orion, highlighting SIMPPLE's continued advancement in the facilities management sector.
news.headline: SIMPPLE Ltd. debuts new multi-functional robot "Orion" with real-time security surveillance and cleaning capabilities in Melbourne.

news.summary: In March, there was greater-than-expected easing of price pressures, bringing relief concerning trade tariffs. This was fueled by President Trump's 90-day tariff pause, boosting investor risk appetite. The annual inflation rate dipped to 2.4%, a six-month low, down from February's 2.8%. Data from the Bureau of Labor Statistics on Thursday revealed this figure was below the anticipated 2.6%. The Consumer Price Index saw a 0.1% monthly contraction, compared to the previous month's 0.2% increase and against forecasts of a 0.1% rise. This is the lowest inflation reading since May 2020. While gasoline prices fell by 6.3%, this was offset by hikes in electricity and natural gas indexes. Food prices, however, rose by 0.4% in March.
news.headline: March inflation rate falls to 2.4%, lowest in 6 months, easing price pressures and boosting hopes for Fed rate cuts.

news.summary: On Tuesday, the Dow Jones surged by 1,300 points following the news of Trump's tariffs. The stock market saw a rally in Nvidia and Tesla shares today. Apple, Nvidia, and Tesla all experienced a boost in their stock prices. Overall, investor confidence was high as indicated by the significant increase in Dow Jones.
news.headline: Dow Jones surged 1,300 points on Trump tariff news, boosting stock markets. Apple, Nvidia, Tesla saw strong rally.

news.summary: Despite a softer inflation report and a temporary trade truce, U.S. Treasury yields rose on Thursday with the 30-year yield up by 8 basis points to 4.81% and the 10-year yield up by 3 basis points to 4.35%. This rise indicates bond investors' skepticism of a near-term Fed pivot and a preparation for prolonged policy uncertainty. The iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond ETF (NASDAQ: TLT) fell 1.5% at the open. The Consumer Price Index (CPI) dropped more than expected in March 2025, leading to hopes of a potential Fed rate cut. Annual inflation decreased to 2.4%, the lowest since September 2024, down from 2.8% in February, with monthly inflation contracting by 0.1%, the first negative reading since May 2020. Bond investors are awaiting a clearer signal from the central bank, known as the "Fed Put."
news.headline: Despite softer inflation data and trade truce, US Treasury yields rose on Thu. 30-year yield up 8 bps to 4.81%, 10-year up 3 bps to 4.35%. Expects signal from Fed on rate cuts. Inflation dropped in Mar 2025, CPI dropped more than expected, annual inflation at 2.4%, lowest since Sep 2024. Core inflation contracted by 0.1% monthly. Bond investors wary, waiting on Fed action.

news.summary: This recent development signifies the largest financing arrangement for a Municipal Utility District in its history. The substantial sum involved underscores the significance of this receivable financing for the district.
news.headline: Texas Infrastructure program secures $196M in bonds for Starwood Land, largest MUD receivable financing.

news.summary: In a dramatic turn of events on Thursday, the Dow Jones Industrial Average plummeted by a staggering 900 points following an unexpected Consumer Price Index (CPI) inflation report. The stock market experienced a notable sell-off of shares from prominent companies like Nvidia and Tesla. This sudden downturn in the market was a result of the surprising inflation data, catching many investors off guard. Overall, this news sent shockwaves through the financial world as traders reacted to the new information.
news.headline: Dow Jones drops 900 points due to surprise inflation report, Nvidia and Tesla stocks sold off.

news.summary: Stay updated on the latest news related to Apple products, including the iPhone and Mac, as well as stock information. Get stock quotes and in-depth chart analysis for Apple Inc. (AAPL) to make informed investment decisions. For comprehensive company updates and financial data, visit Investor's Business Daily for valuable insights.
news.headline: Get latest news on Apple products & stock quotes (AAPL) plus chart analysis on Investor's Business Daily.

news.summary: According to BofA Securities analyst Rafe Jadrosich, building product companies could face challenges from tariffs, with the recent proposal of increased tariffs by the Trump administration affecting various countries. The impact of these tariffs remains uncertain. Specifically, USMCA-compliant goods from Mexico and Canada will have unchanged rates. Companies like Fortune Brands Innovations, Inc. (NYSE: FBIN) and Masco Corp (NYSE: MAS) may be at risk due to their production relocation to Southeast Asia and their continued ties to China. This poses a threat to these plumbing firms, as noted by the analyst. In addition, Mohawk Industries, Inc. (NYSE: MHK) and Whirlpool Corp (NYSE: WHR) may also be impacted. Full details are available on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: BofA Securities analyst warns building product firms like Fortune Brands, Masco face tariff risks due to production in Southeast Asia and exposure to China. More on Benzinga.com.

news.summary: In Rochester, New York, on April 10, 2025, Syntec Optics Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: OPTX), a prominent technology solutions provider for defense, communications, biomedical, and consumer sectors, disclosed that they have received follow-up orders amounting to $2.1M. These orders are for crucial optics components designed to be integrated into cutting-edge military scopes.
news.headline: Syntec Optics (Nasdaq: OPTX) secures new $2.1M advanced optics order for U.S. defense integrated scopes.

news.summary: Business organizations are striving to sway the Trump administration as they aim to deter the implementation of a substantial port fee on Chinese-manufactured vessels docking at U.S. ports. Through hearings scheduled for this week, they intend to emphasize the detrimental impact of the proposed fee on both businesses and the economy. The fee amounts to a substantial sum, with estimates indicating it could reach millions of dollars annually. The business groups hope to showcase that this fee could have adverse effects on trade relations and potentially spark retaliatory measures from China.
news.headline: Business groups are urging USTR to reject a port fee on Chinese-made ships; seeking to persuade Trump administration in hearings this week.

news.summary: Shift recently interviewed Erica Groshen, former Commissioner of the Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS), to discuss the implications of eliminating two advisory groups on the country's job data. Groshen provided insights into how this decision may impact the accuracy and reliability of key employment figures, such as the national unemployment rate and job growth numbers. With this move, the BLS may face challenges in ensuring the quality of its data and maintaining transparency in reporting essential labor market statistics. As an expert in labor economics, Groshen's perspective sheds light on the potential consequences of restructuring advisory mechanisms within the BLS. This development raises concerns about the future integrity of job-related data that policymakers, businesses, and the public rely on for making informed decisions.
news.headline: Former BLS Commissioner warns about impact of cutting two advisory committees on job data, raises concerns.

news.summary: The Apple iPhone, along with PCs, servers, and various tech gear from companies like Nvidia and Super Micro, have been excluded from significant tariffs by President Trump. This move has been regarded as extremely positive news for the tech industry and has garnered approval from various sectors.
news.headline: Trump exempts Apple iPhone and other tech gear from tariffs, benefiting companies like Nvidia and Super Micro.

news.summary: Co-founder of BitMEX, Arthur Hayes, has predicted a notable surge in the value of Bitcoin (BTC) due to President Donald Trump's tariff policies. Hayes pointed out that the selling of US treasuries and the increase in 10-year bond yields could potentially result in more money printing. This has historically led to significant rallies in Bitcoin. Additionally, Hayes mentioned that the devaluation of China's yuan as a result of central bank money printing may prompt investors to shift their assets into Bitcoin as a form of protection. For more details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Arthur Hayes predicts Bitcoin surge due to US tariff policies, speculates on money printing impact. Hayes suggests devaluation of China's yuan could drive investor interest in Bitcoin as a safe haven. (includes numbers: predicting significant increase in Bitcoin value)

news.summary: The US Social Security Administration (SSA) is shifting all public communications to the X social media platform following recommendations from the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) led by Elon Musk. The SSA is downsizing its workforce from 57,000 employees to around 50,000 to cut costs and enhance efficiency by reducing its regional structure to four regions. Musk has criticized the SSA for wrongful payments, sparking debates on sustainable government spending. DOGE is aiming to cut 20% of the Internal Revenue Service's (IRS) workforce, potentially affecting 6,800 employees by May 15. The agency is also exploring blockchain technology to promote transparency in government spending.
news.headline: US Social Security to shift public comms to X amid DOGE-led job cuts, downsizing staff from 57,000 to 50,000. Source: Elon Musk.

news.summary: A recent report revealed that there has been a significant increase in online shopping worldwide. With the ongoing global pandemic, more people have turned to online platforms for their shopping needs. The report states that global e-commerce sales have gone up by 27.6% compared to last year, totaling $4.28 trillion. In the US alone, online sales have surged by 44%, reaching $861.12 billion. This spike in online shopping is predicted to continue even after the pandemic is over, changing the way people shop in the future.
news.headline: Apple and Nvidia granted exemptions from US tariffs. #News #TradeDeal

news.summary: Last week, the market experienced significant fluctuations, with a notable surge. Apple and other electronics companies have recently been granted a major exemption from tariffs, potentially boosting the tech sector on Monday. Palantir stands out as one of the top-performing stocks to keep an eye on. Investors are now speculating whether Apple and other tech companies will rally following the tariff exemption announcement. The exemption has generated significant interest, particularly regarding its potential impact on Dow Jones Futures. This development underscores the importance of monitoring the performance of these seven key stocks in the coming days.
news.headline: Tech stocks, including Apple, may rise after big tariff exemption; Palantir leads 7 stocks to watch. Market saw higher swings last week.

news.summary: In Bengaluru, India on April 12, 2025, it was reported that the Procurement Outsourcing Market has been divided into Direct Procurement and Indirect Procurement types, with applications in the CPG and Retail as well as BFSI sectors. Valued at USD 5577 million in 2024, the market is anticipated to experience growth in the coming years.
news.headline: Global Procurement Outsourcing Market to grow at 11.7% CAGR. Valued at USD 5577M in 2024. Key players: IBM, Accenture, Infosys.
US and Iran agree to meet again next Saturday White House
Iran discussions characterized as positive and constructive by White House

news.summary: The city of London took action to prevent the factory's Chinese owners from shutting down the plant, which could have put numerous jobs at risk and posed a threat to national security.
news.headline: UK government seizes control of final steel mill to avert closure by Chinese owners, safeguarding jobs and security.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the unemployment rate dropped to 4.2%, which is the lowest it has been in the past decade. This decrease in unemployment represents approximately 1.8 million job seekers who have found employment. Additionally, the report showed a significant increase in job creation, with 450,000 new jobs added in the past month alone. The economy also experienced growth of 5.6% in the last quarter, exceeding expectations and indicating a strong recovery from the recent downturn. This positive trend is expected to continue, offering hope for further improvement in the job market and overall economic stability.
news.headline: Apple and Nvidia are exempted from US tariffs, providing relief to both companies.

news.summary: Last month, a record number of 3,000 new cases of a rare disease were reported in a small town. The local hospital treated 1,500 patients, with 500 of them requiring intensive care. The outbreak prompted the authorities to deploy 200 medical personnel to assist with the situation. Additionally, 100 temporary hospital beds were set up to accommodate the increasing number of patients. The disease has a mortality rate of 10%, and so far, 50 deaths have been reported.Officials are working to identify the source of the outbreak and implement necessary measures to control the spread of the disease.
news.headline: Lawsuit: Harvard professors sue Trump over $9B funds threat.

news.summary: President Trump has announced a 90-day pause on tariffs for countries that haven't retaliated against U.S. trade measures, causing market rise, particularly in tech stocks. He received criticism from Michigan auto and business groups for maintaining automotive import tariffs. U.S. and Vietnam have started trade negotiations after Trump paused a 46% tariff on Vietnam's exports, benefiting the country's tech manufacturing sector. EU and China are considering minimum prices for Chinese electric vehicles instead of last year's tariffs. Q1 2025 EV sales show growth, suggesting a strong domestic sector. EU could impose retaliatory measures on U.S. tech companies like levies on digital ad revenues. Microsoft fired two employees for disrupting its 50th-anniversary event.
news.headline: President Trump announces 90-day tariff pause, Vietnam trade negotiations commence, EU-China explore EV tariffs alternative, Microsoft may cut jobs.

news.summary: The recent report highlighted a surge in unemployment numbers, with the rate increasing by 2.5% to reach 6.7%. This significant rise adds up to a total of 10.3 million individuals currently unemployed. In addition, the report also pointed out a decrease in GDP growth by 4.2% compared to last year, signaling economic challenges. Despite these concerning figures, there is optimism regarding the upcoming stimulus package of $1.9 trillion aimed at boosting the economy and aiding those in need. This package includes direct payments of $1,400 to eligible individuals and an extension of unemployment benefits to provide relief during these tough times. Experts believe that with the implementation of such measures, there is hope for a gradual economic recovery in the near future.
news.headline: Harvard professors file lawsuit against Trump over $9B funding threat.

news.summary: In a recent development, President Trump has decided to exempt the Apple iPhone, as well as PCs, servers, and various tech equipment from companies like Nvidia and Super Micro, from the majority of tariffs. This decision comes as a relief for these companies as they will not have to face the burden of significant tariffs on their products. The exemption extends to a variety of tech gear, marking a positive move for the tech industry. The decision has been welcomed by investors and is expected to have a beneficial impact on the companies involved.
news.headline: Trump exempts Apple, Nvidia, Super Micro & others from tariffs, including iPhones, PCs, servers. Big win for tech industry.

news.summary: President Donald Trump has exempted tech products such as smartphones, chips, computers, and select electronics from ongoing trade tariffs, as per the US Customs and Border Protection. This move is seen as a respite for the tech industry, with items like storage cards, modems, diodes, and semiconductors also excluded. The exemption is expected to benefit large-cap tech companies, easing pressure on tech stocks which suffered due to the trade war. Following the announcement, Bitcoin surpassed $85,000 on April 12, indicating a positive response from crypto markets to the tariff exemptions. Trump's tariff policies have been seen as a negotiation tool for a US-China trade deal. The yield on the 10-year US Treasury Bond spiked to approximately 4.5% on April 11, showing bond investors' reaction to the macroeconomic uncertainty caused by the trade war. Bitcoin advocate Max Keiser believes these exemptions won't impact bond yields significantly. Keiser raised concerns about a declining confidence in US bonds and the US Dollar. This article advises readers to do their own research before making investment decisions and does not offer any recommendations.
news.headline: Trump exempts tech products from tariffs, aiding crypto? US President spares smartphones, chips, computers, electronics from tariffs. Tariff exemptions boost tech stocks, Bitcoin surpasses $85,000. Tariff pause triggers 9% surge in Bitcoin, 10% in S&P 500. US Treasury bond yield hits 4.5%. Trade war complicates macroeconomic scene. Exclusion of tech items may not ease bond yields. News stirs conflict of interest concerns.
Miller notes Chinese electronics imports still subject to 20% tariff
Trump: Exemption is retroactive to April 5
Trump: White House Statement Comments
